original_abstract: The concept of piety (taqwa) serves as a fundamental pillar of faith, shaping the character and conduct of a devout Muslim. This research investigates the methods for attaining and manifesting taqwa through the lens of Buya Hamka’s "Tafsir Al-Azhar." Utilizing a qualitative library research methodology, this study examines Hamka’s primary work alongside secondary academic sources, including relevant journals and scientific texts. The findings reveal that Hamka perceives taqwa as more than a mere avoidance of divine retribution; rather, it encompasses a proactive spirit of devotion characterized by an increase in righteous deeds and the cultivation of compassion toward humanity. The research concludes that the implementation of taqwa is dual-faceted, involving "habluminallah" (maintaining a steadfast relationship with Allah through obedience) and "hablumminnas" (fostering social harmony through empathy and care for others). By integrating these vertical and horizontal dimensions, an individual can achieve a comprehensive state of piety that aligns with the ethical and spiritual objectives of the Qur'an.
humanized_abstract: This paper examined the ways to attain and manifest taqwa, the major faith factor in a Muslim’s personal trait as the concept of piety. This qualitative library research examined Buya Hamka’s primary works and other secondary data from journals and other scientific texts. From Hamka’s exegesis, Tafsir Al-Azhar, the concept of taqwa was found to be more than an aversive concept, a concept that keeps man away from the wrath of God. Rather, taqwa as seen through Hamka’s lens is an appetitive aspect, a man’s desire to oblige Allah and by doing so, does good deeds, while on the other hand, having taqwa means having empathy towards human beings. Thus, in practice, taqwa has two sides namely the aspect of hob and maqable to it, the “habluminallah” and the “hablumminnas” which by combining both vertical and horizontal aspects of taqwa, thus aims to attain a higher level of spirituality that meets the purposes of the Quran.
